page: Amazon Web Services hpc7a.96xlarge is a high-performance computing instance powered by the AWS Nitro System. It features 192 dedicated physical cores utilizing the x86_64 architecture with AMD EPYC 9R14 processors running at 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 768 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 4 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but features a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the hpc7a.96xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, scoring in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, PassMark CPU score, and Geekbench multi-core metrics. Its memory architecture delivers high throughput, with read-only large block memory bandwidth reaching 704.22 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 345.83 GB/sec. The instance also excels in parallel processing tasks, such as multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, software compilation, and Redis SET operations, which registered 408.36 million operations per second. Single-threaded performance is comparatively moderate, with single-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores falling in the top 25% bracket.

Given its high density of dedicated physical cores and DDR5 memory bandwidth, this instance offers high qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized, compute-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The absence of local storage means storage requirements must be handled via network-attached options. The hpc7a.96xlarge is highly suited for high-performance computing applications, large-scale scientific simulations, multi-threaded software compilation, and compute-heavy parallel processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services hpc7a.96xlarge is a high-performance computing instance featuring 192 dedicated physical cores powered by AMD EPYC 9R14 processors running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 768 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, delivering 4 GB per core, alongside a 150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance does not feature local storage or GPUs. In benchmarks, the hpc7a.96xlarge delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high throughput for parallel tasks like software compilation and Redis operations. It represents a resource-dense option for compute-heavy workloads, offering high qualitative efficiency for parallel processing. It is optimized for high-performance computing, scientific simulations, and multi-threaded engineering workloads.

tagline: A high-density, dedicated physical core instance featuring DDR5 memory and high-bandwidth networking for parallelized high-performance computing workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
768 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory (4 GB per core)
150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Designed for high-performance computing, simulations, and parallel processing
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
